title: Micromeles meghalayensis
taxonomy: Micromeles meghalayensis is a species of plant, specifically a type of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. It belongs to the genus Micromeles. The species Micromeles meghalayensis is native to the state of Meghalaya in India. The classification of this plant places it within the kingdom Plantae and further categorizes it as a member of the class Magnoliopsida. Micromeles meghalayensis is also known as the Meghalaya mehla.

intro: Micromeles meghalayensis, commonly known as the Meghalaya Micromeles, is a type of shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and improve overall immune function. The bioactive constituents of Micromeles meghalayensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
